question: Will China GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 4.5% and 5.0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to China's Y/Y Growth Rate (%) of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for April 17, 2026.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution. 

For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/202512/t20251226_1962154.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 75% implied probability to China GDP growth of 4.5-5.0% YoY in Q1 2026, closely tracking economist forecasts from the IMF and World Bank projecting around 4.3-4.5% for full-year 2026 amid structural headwinds. This positioning stems from Q3 2024's 4.6% print—below the 5% target—bolstered by aggressive stimulus including PBOC rate cuts and fiscal expansion announced in late 2024, aimed at stabilizing property woes and consumption. Downside risks from potential U.S. tariffs and high local debt cap enthusiasm for higher bins at just 0.9-0.3%, while key Q4 2024 data and 2025 policy execution will refine these market-implied odds.

question: Will Trove refund ICO participants?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Trove publicly confirms and executes refunds to ICO participants totaling at least $7,000,000 by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting, official statements from Trove, or on-chain evidence confirming refunds.

question: Will Alberto Pimentel win Big Brother Brasil 26?
description:
This market will resolve to the contestant who wins Big Brother Brasil 26.

If Big Brother Brasil 26 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Big Brother Brasil 26 has otherwise not concluded by April 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". In the case of a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed contestant whose name comes first in alphabetical order. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of the final episode of Big Brother Brasil 26.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ana Paula Renault commands a commanding 70% implied probability to win Big Brother Brasil 26, fueled by her enduring fanbase from BBB15 and rampant social media campaigns positioning her as the comeback queen. Trader sentiment solidified after recent leaks and insider buzz on Globo forums naming her among top rumored returnees, alongside strong box-office-like popularity metrics from fan polls. Jonas Sulzbach trails at 16.8% on nostalgia from his BBB15 runner-up run and alliance-building rep, while others like Chaiany Andrade linger below 5% amid weaker pre-season hype. With the full cast announcement looming mid-year, volatility persists as historical BBB voting patterns reward viral moments over early odds.

If Big Brother Brasil 26 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Big Brother Brasil 26 has otherwise not concluded by April 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". In the case of a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed contestant whose name comes first in alphabetical order. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of the final episode of Big Brother Brasil 26.

Trader sentiment solidified after recent leaks and insider buzz on Globo forums naming her among top rumored returnees, alongside strong box-office-like popularity metrics from fan polls. Jonas Sulzbach trails at 16.8% on nostalgia from his BBB15 runner-up run and alliance-building rep, while others like Chaiany Andrade linger below 5% amid weaker pre-season hype. With the full cast announcement looming mid-year, volatility persists as historical BBB voting patterns reward viral moments over early odds.

question: Will Justin Sun meet with Elon Musk in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Justin Sun meets with Elon Musk by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Justin Sun and Elon Musk are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Justin Sun meet with Elon Musk in 2026?

question: Huddle FDV above $20M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Huddle's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Huddle (https://x.com/huddle01com) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Kanye West and Bianca Censori separate in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kanye West and/or Bianca Censori announce their intention to legally separate or divorce between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET of their intention to separate or divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the separation or divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual separation or divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Kanye West, Bianca Censori, and/or their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Kanye West and Bianca Censori staying together through 2026 at 68.5% implied probability for "No," driven primarily by their consistent public appearances amid swirling tabloid rumors, including joint outings in Tokyo and Los Angeles as recently as October 2024, signaling ongoing unity despite Kanye's polarizing antics and Vultures 2 rollout. While unverified divorce whispers peaked in February over alleged tensions, no legal filings or official statements confirm strife, echoing patterns in celebrity couples where spectacle overshadows substance. Historical precedent from Kanye's prior marriages adds caution, but Bianca's steadfast presence through controversies bolsters stability bets, with traders eyeing 2025 album cycles as potential flashpoints absent concrete catalysts for split.

question: SCOTUS accepts sports event contract case by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Supreme Court of the United States grants certiorari in a case explicitly concerning the legality, regulation, or jurisdictional authority over sports event contracts by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A case qualifies if it addresses at least one of the following: (1) whether contracts based on sporting event outcomes constitute regulated derivatives under the Commodity Exchange Act; (2) whether federal regulation via the Commodity Futures Trading Commission preempts state-level gambling laws as applied to such contracts; or (3) whether sports event contracts offered by federally licensed markets may legally be offered, restricted, or prohibited by federal or state authorities.

The certiorari grant must be publicly confirmed via the official SCOTUS docket or orders list, and verifiable through credible legal reporting or the Supreme Court’s official website. The case does not need to be heard, scheduled, or decided to qualify. 

The resolution source will be a consensus census of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on SCOTUS granting certiorari in the sports event contract dispute reflects the Court's historically low 1% petition acceptance rate, with current odds implying under 5% probability by the deadline. The case, stemming from a federal appeals court ruling on contract enforceability for event tickets, lacks a clear circuit split—a key factor for review—despite arguments over arbitration clauses and consumer protections. Recent development: Petition filed October 2024, with respondent briefs due mid-November and initial conference likely December. Upcoming Orders List on November 22 and December 6 could signal early denial or relist, while broader Court docket congestion from election-related matters tempers optimism among bettors.

question: Will Russia join the Board of Peace?
description:
Donald Trump and the United States recently sent invitations to countries around the world, inviting them to join the US-led Board of Peace which will oversee conflict resolution in Gaza and elsewhere (see: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/us-names-rubio-blair-kushner-gaza-board-under-trumps-plan-2026-01-17/).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country joins the Board of Peace by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A country will be considered to have joined the Board of Peace if either of the following conditions are met:

- That country's government publicly and definitively announces that the country will join the Board of Peace as a founding member, permanent member, or as any other type of member state.

- Official information from the country’s government or a consensus of credible reporting demonstrates that the listed country has formally joined the Board of Peace (e.g. an authorized representative has signed the charter of the Board of Peace).

Only statements or announcements which are definitive in their expression of intent to join the Board of Peace will qualify (e.g. “we agree to join the board of peace,” “we accepted an invitation to the board of peace,” etc.). Statements which are not definitive in their expression of intent to join (e.g. “we agree in principle”, “we will join pending further details”, “we are ready to do our part in working for the reconstruction of Gaza” etc.) will not count. 

Qualifying announcements within this market’s timeframe will count regardless of when the relevant country intends to join the board of peace, or whether or not they ultimately join.

Statements from Donald Trump or the US government will not alone qualify.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the listed country; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows slim odds for any countries joining the Board of Peace by March 31, reflecting the absence of official announcements or diplomatic breakthroughs from potential participants like regional mediators or neutral powers. No governments have publicly confirmed intentions, with recent diplomatic talks on global conflict resolution—such as UN Security Council sessions and bilateral summits—yielding no specific commitments to this body. Uncertainty stems from geopolitical tensions, including ongoing wars in Ukraine and the Middle East, which prioritize bilateral ceasefires over new institutional boards. Traders eye upcoming events like the late-March G20 preparatory meetings and potential EU foreign ministers' discussions, which could signal shifts, but base rates from similar unfulfilled peace initiatives suggest low resolution likelihood.

question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 40% and 44.9%?
description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.title: Argentina Annual Inflation 2026
events.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' tight clustering of odds around 20-35% for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation—led by 20-24.9% at 28.5%, 25-29.9% at 25.5%, and 30-34.9% at 22.1%—signals strong consensus on President Milei's disinflation reforms sustaining momentum beyond 2025. Recent monthly CPI eased to 2.4% in October from 3.5% prior, dragging yearly inflation to 193% from peaks above 270%, while IMF projections peg end-2025 at ~29% and central bank targets imply sub-25% for 2026 amid fiscal surpluses and a crawling peso peg. Competitive dynamics hinge on fiscal discipline versus shocks like commodity slumps or drought; resolution thresholds favor aggressive bets on 25-30% if Q1 2025 prints hold below 2.5% monthly, underscoring capital-weighted trader bets on policy endurance over historical volatility.

question: Will the official USD to ARS exchange rate be less than 1250.00 at the end of December 2026?
description:
This is a market about Argentina’s official wholesale U.S. dollar exchange rate at market close on the last business day of December 2026, as published by the Central Bank of Argentina (BCRA).

This market will resolve according to the official wholesale USD to ARS exchange rate (Tipo de Cambio Mayorista, ($ por USD) Com. A 3500 | Referencia) at market close on the final business day of December 2026, as published by the BCRA on its official website (https://www.bcra.gob.ar/).

If the official rate for that date has not been published by the end of the 7th day after the end of the specified month, the market will resolve according to the most recently published official wholesale rate preceding that date.

The resolution source for this market will be the official BCRA publication. Resolution will occur once this figure is available.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the Central Bank of Argentina (BCRA), which reports the Wholesale Exchange Rate to two decimal points (e.g., 1,408.02 ARS per USD). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 54% implied probability to Argentina's official USD/ARS exchange rate surpassing 1600 by end-2026, reflecting expectations of cumulative inflation outpacing the central bank's crawling peg despite President Milei's fiscal reforms. The BCRA's official rate stands at approximately 980 ARS/USD as of late 2024, with monthly devaluations of 1-2% amid reserves hovering near $30 billion gross but net negative positions. Recent catalysts include November's 2.4% monthly inflation print—the lowest in years per INDEC—and Milei's allies' midterm electoral gains, bolstering austerity and deregulation prospects. However, dollar shortages and IMF negotiations introduce devaluation risks, positioning 1500-1599.99 (27.1% combined) as the next likely band amid uncertain peso stabilization.

question: Tulane Green Wave vs. Memphis Tigers (W)
description:
In the upcoming WBB game, scheduled for January 27 at 8:00 PM ET:

If the Tulane Green Wave win, the market will resolve to "Tulane Green Wave".

If the Memphis Tigers win, the market will resolve to "Memphis Tigers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Tulane Green Wave vs. Memphis Tigers (W)
events.description:
This event is for the WBB game between Tulane Green Wave and Memphis Tigers on January 27 at 8:00 PM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tulane Green Wave's dominant home performance at Fogelman Arena drives the 63% implied probability favoring them over Memphis Tigers in this AAC women's basketball matchup. The Wave boast a 7-1 home record this season, riding a four-game win streak with balanced scoring from guards like Chiara Hunter (15.2 PPG) and improved defense holding foes under 65 points lately. Memphis struggles on the road (2-5), hampered by sophomore forward Madison Grubbs' questionable ankle status per latest injury report, which could thin their frontcourt depth. Head-to-head, Tulane swept last year's series, capitalizing on transition play where Memphis ranks bottom-third in AAC turnover margin. Trader consensus reflects these edges amid Tulane's push for postseason positioning.

question: Will Lương Cường be the next President of Vietnam?
description:
Vietnam’s President is selected through internal decisions of the Communist Party of Vietnam and its National Assembly. The Party is holding its 14th National Congress from January 19 to January 25, 2026, after which the National Assembly of Vietnam is expected to elect leadership positions including President.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is formally elected by the National Assembly to assume the office of President of Vietnam.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected to assume the office of President. Any acting, interim, or caretaker President will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If the offices of President of Vietnam and General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam are merged such that an individual is elected to hold a joint office as leader of the party and head of state of Vietnam, this market will resolve to that individual.

If no such President is elected by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the National Assembly of Vietnam; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tô Lâm's commanding 91.5% trader consensus as Vietnam's next president stems from his unchallenged position as Communist Party General Secretary—the paramount leadership role—and his recent unanimous election as State President by the National Assembly on October 21, 2024, with all 478 votes. This dual authority consolidates power amid the ongoing anti-corruption campaign he spearheads, sidelining rivals and ensuring party loyalty. Lower odds on figures like National Assembly Chairman Trần Thanh Mẫn or Defense Minister Phan Văn Giang reflect their subordinate roles. Realistic challenges include unforeseen health issues for Tô Lâm or abrupt Politburo shifts before the 2026 Party Congress, though current evidence points to continuity.

question: Will the New York Yankees win the 2026 World Series?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB World Series. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLB World Series per the rules of MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from MLB (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: MLB World Series Champion 2026
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB World Series. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLB World Series per the rules of MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from MLB (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Los Angeles Dodgers command a 27.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorites for the 2026 World Series, fueled by their 2024 championship core of Shohei Ohtani, Mookie Betts, Freddie Freeman and a deep rotation including Yoshinobu Yamamoto and Tyler Glasnow, positioning them for sustained dominance amid minimal free-agent losses. Seattle Mariners (9.3%) differentiate via MLB's elite starting pitching staff—led by Luis Castillo, Logan Gilbert and George Kirby—offering rotation stability despite offensive questions. New York Yankees (7.5%) and Mets (6.0%) rely on Aaron Judge and Juan Soto's star power, with the Mets boosted by Soto's blockbuster extension, while Toronto Blue Jays, Boston Red Sox and Atlanta Braves cluster around 5.5% on young talent surges, trade acquisitions like Garrett Crochet for Boston, and Atlanta's recovering injury-plagued lineup, underscoring a wide-open field beyond the Dodgers' edge.

question: Bill Clinton charged by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Bill Clinton between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Norway join the Board of Peace?
description:
Donald Trump and the United States recently sent invitations to countries around the world, inviting them to join the US-led Board of Peace which will oversee conflict resolution in Gaza and elsewhere (see: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/us-names-rubio-blair-kushner-gaza-board-under-trumps-plan-2026-01-17/).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country joins the Board of Peace by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A country will be considered to have joined the Board of Peace if either of the following conditions are met:

- That country's government publicly and definitively announces that the country will join the Board of Peace as a founding member, permanent member, or as any other type of member state.

- Official information from the country’s government or a consensus of credible reporting demonstrates that the listed country has formally joined the Board of Peace (e.g. an authorized representative has signed the charter of the Board of Peace).

Only statements or announcements which are definitive in their expression of intent to join the Board of Peace will qualify (e.g. “we agree to join the board of peace,” “we accepted an invitation to the board of peace,” etc.). Statements which are not definitive in their expression of intent to join (e.g. “we agree in principle”, “we will join pending further details”, “we are ready to do our part in working for the reconstruction of Gaza” etc.) will not count. 

Qualifying announcements within this market’s timeframe will count regardless of when the relevant country intends to join the board of peace, or whether or not they ultimately join.

Statements from Donald Trump or the US government will not alone qualify.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the listed country; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which countries will join the Board of Peace by March 31?
events.description:
Donald Trump and the United States recently sent invitations to countries around the world, inviting them to join the US-led Board of Peace which will oversee conflict resolution in Gaza and elsewhere (see: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/us-names-rubio-blair-kushner-gaza-board-under-trumps-plan-2026-01-17/).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country joins the Board of Peace by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A country will be considered to have joined the Board of Peace if either of the following conditions are met:

- That country's government publicly and definitively announces that the country will join the Board of Peace as a founding member, permanent member, or as any other type of member state.

- Official information from the country’s government or a consensus of credible reporting demonstrates that the listed country has formally joined the Board of Peace (e.g. an authorized representative has signed the charter of the Board of Peace).

Only statements or announcements which are definitive in their expression of intent to join the Board of Peace will qualify (e.g. “we agree to join the board of peace,” “we accepted an invitation to the board of peace,” etc.). Statements which are not definitive in their expression of intent to join (e.g. “we agree in principle”, “we will join pending further details”, “we are ready to do our part in working for the reconstruction of Gaza” etc.) will not count. 

Qualifying announcements within this market’s timeframe will count regardless of when the relevant country intends to join the board of peace, or whether or not they ultimately join.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the listed country; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows slim odds for any countries joining the Board of Peace by March 31, reflecting the absence of official announcements or diplomatic breakthroughs from potential participants like regional mediators or neutral powers. No governments have publicly confirmed intentions, with recent diplomatic talks on global conflict resolution—such as UN Security Council sessions and bilateral summits—yielding no specific commitments to this body. Uncertainty stems from geopolitical tensions, including ongoing wars in Ukraine and the Middle East, which prioritize bilateral ceasefires over new institutional boards. Traders eye upcoming events like the late-March G20 preparatory meetings and potential EU foreign ministers' discussions, which could signal shifts, but base rates from similar unfulfilled peace initiatives suggest low resolution likelihood.

- That country's government publicly and definitively announces that the country will join the Board of Peace as a founding member, permanent member, or as any other type of member state.

- Official information from the country’s government or a consensus of credible reporting demonstrates that the listed country has formally joined the Board of Peace (e.g. an authorized representative has signed the charter of the Board of Peace).

Only statements or announcements which are definitive in their expression of intent to join the Board of Peace will qualify (e.g. “we agree to join the board of peace,” “we accepted an invitation to the board of peace,” etc.). Statements which are not definitive in their expression of intent to join (e.g. “we agree in principle”, “we will join pending further details”, “we are ready to do our part in working for the reconstruction of Gaza” etc.) will not count. 

Qualifying announcements within this market’s timeframe will count regardless of when the relevant country intends to join the board of peace, or whether or not they ultimately join.

Statements from Donald Trump or the US government will not alone qualify.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the listed country; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows slim odds for any countries joining the Board of Peace by March 31, reflecting the absence of official announcements or diplomatic breakthroughs from potential participants like regional mediators or neutral powers. No governments have publicly confirmed intentions, with recent diplomatic talks on global conflict resolution—such as UN Security Council sessions and bilateral summits—yielding no specific commitments to this body. Uncertainty stems from geopolitical tensions, including ongoing wars in Ukraine and the Middle East, which prioritize bilateral ceasefires over new institutional boards. Traders eye upcoming events like the late-March G20 preparatory meetings and potential EU foreign ministers' discussions, which could signal shifts, but base rates from similar unfulfilled peace initiatives suggest low resolution likelihood.

question: Trump x Greenland deal signed by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if both Denmark and the United States sign a deal, treaty, or similar international agreement of any kind relating to Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any U.S.–Danish agreement relating to Greenland will qualify, regardless of subject matter, including but not limited to sovereignty, governance, security arrangements, or access to natural resources.

Examples of qualifying deals include but are not limited to a treaty that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession (even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement treaty establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission; or agreements permitting additional U.S. troop stationing, basing access, or resource extraction rights in Greenland.

This market will resolve to “Yes” only if a qualifying agreement is formally signed by authorized representatives of both Denmark and the United States. Official announcements, statements of intent, or declarations that an agreement has been reached will not suffice unless accompanied by signatures from both sides. Whether or not a qualifying deal is later passed by the respective parliaments or enters into force will not affect this market’s resolution. Signaling from Greenland’s population will not be considered.

Announcements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or understandings that are not formally signed by both parties will not qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction, control, basing rights, or access arrangements in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying agreements.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Donald Trump's 2024 election victory has reignited trader optimism for a Greenland acquisition deal by December 31, reflected in the 62% implied probability on Yes shares, as his past advocacy for the strategic territory underscores potential U.S. national security priorities. During his first term, Trump publicly floated purchasing the Danish autonomous region, citing Arctic resources and military basing, though Denmark firmly rejected the overture. Recent post-election buzz, including Trump's reiterations of interest on social media and endorsements from allies like Elon Musk, fuels speculation of accelerated diplomacy despite the president-elect's limited authority pre-inauguration. No official negotiations are confirmed, and Greenlandic leaders emphasize sovereignty, tempering expectations amid the compressed timeline. Traders weigh these dynamics against historical diplomatic hurdles.

question: Will Trump act to ban mail-in voting or voting machines by June 30?
description:
On August 18, 2025, President Donald J. Trump announced his commitment to eliminate mail-in voting and voting machines in U.S. elections, ahead of the 2026 midterms.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump signs any federal legislation or performs any executive action that inhibits mail-in voting or voting machines from use for the 2026 midterm election by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any executive action or legislation that restricts, limits, or prohibits the use of mail-in voting or voting machines in any way will qualify, even if the action is delayed, suspended, or subsequently blocked by judicial or other actions.

This market’s primary resolution source will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible information from major news outlets or government statements will also be used.

Recent catalysts include his nominations of election integrity advocates to key DOJ and FBI roles, such as Kash Patel, and RNC pledges under Lara Trump to litigate voting irregularities aggressively. No formal ban announcement has surfaced from the president-elect's transition, yet market pricing captures trader consensus on swift reforms ahead of 2026 midterms, tempered by statutory limits on federal overreach and state election control.

question: Will Canada’s 2026 inflation be between 3.0% and 3.4%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices over the 12-month period ending December 2026 in Canada, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by Statistics Canada.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly Statistics Canada report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Statistics Canada Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.statcan.gc.ca/en/start), currently scheduled to be released on January 18, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

Once available, you can find this report by clicking on the "Major Economic Indicators" heading on the home page of https://www.statcan.gc.ca/en/start and selecting the “Consumer Price Index” report for the relevant month. The relevant figure can be found in “Table 1” under the "% change" column for the relevant month and year compared to the same month of the previous year.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Statistics Canada CPI news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/n1/dai-quo/cal1-eng.htm?sk=3665

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders see Canada's 2026 annual inflation locked in a tight contest, with 2.0-2.4% edging out at 23.5% probability, closely trailed by 3.0-3.4% (20.2%) and 2.5-2.9% (19.0%), reflecting balanced sentiment around the Bank of Canada's 2% target. Recent November CPI printed 1.9% headline year-over-year—its lowest since early 2021—but core gauges lingered near 3%, sustaining upside risks from persistent shelter costs (up 5.1%) and wage growth exceeding 4%. Key differentiators include January's BoC rate decision amid 3.75% policy rate, volatile oil prices impacting the commodity exporter, and immigration policy shifts curbing demand pressures; a sub-2% trajectory hinges on further disinflation, while global trade tensions could entrench 3%+ trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a fragmented outlook for UK annual inflation in 2026, with 4.5%+ leading at 29% implied probability amid sticky services inflation near 5% and robust wage growth exceeding 5%, closely rivaling 1.5-2.4% bands totaling 74% combined but split across buckets. Primary drivers include the Bank of England's cautious rate cuts—now at 4.5%—against fiscal expansion from the Autumn Budget boosting demand pressures, while global energy volatility adds upside risk. Key differentiators hinge on upcoming CPI releases through mid-2025 and BoE's February Inflation Report; persistent core pressures above 3% could solidify high-end odds, but recessionary GDP drags might validate sub-2% trader bets.

question: Will MoistCr1TiKaL get a haircut in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if MoistCr1TiKaL (penguinz0) gets a haircut in 2026 by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “haircut” refers to a clearly visible and noticeable shortening to his hair, small adjustments of an inch or two will not qualify unless they are a clear result of a haircut. 

Taking a little snip or minor trim on stream (or in a video) also does not count. It must be a real, substantial haircut that noticeably changes his appearance from the long hair style he's maintained.

The resolution source for this market will be photographic or video evidence.

question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.8% and 3.0%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Eurozone annual inflation for 2026 reflects a tight contest between persistent upside risks and disinflationary forces, with market-implied odds favoring 3.1%+ at 42.1% and 2.8-3.0% at 37.1% over lower bands like 1.6-1.8% at 32.0%. Primary drivers include sticky services inflation and robust wage growth—Eurostat data shows November HICP at 2.3% YoY—bolstered by ECB's December staff projections of 1.9% for 2026, which traders view as optimistic amid geopolitical energy shocks and potential U.S. tariff spillovers. Differentiating factors hinge on ECB rate path efficacy versus fiscal loosening; upcoming March CPI and ECB March meeting will be pivotal, as probabilities imply trader consensus clusters above the 2% target.

question: Will China's annual inflation in 2026 be between 2.0% and 2.4%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in China over the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) of China.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) during the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly NBS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the NBS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/), currently expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find the relevant figure by locating the report for December 2026 on the Press Releases page (see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/), locating the table named "Consumer Price Indexes in December 2026", and finding the consumer price index figure in the column labeled "Growth Rate Y/Y (%)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly NBS CPI news release which reports inflation during 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for China's 2026 annual inflation clusters tightly around subdued outcomes, with 0.1-0.5% at 16% implied probability edging out 1.1-1.5% (15%) and 2.5%+ (14.4%), signaling trader consensus on persistent lowflation risks. Driving this are August 2024 CPI readings of -0.5% year-over-year—marking five straight months of deflation—fueled by property sector distress, tepid consumer spending, and excess capacity, despite PBOC rate cuts and fiscal stimulus pledges. Forward projections from major institutions anticipate a gradual pickup to 1-2% amid potential demand recovery, but competitive dynamics hinge on stimulus transmission efficacy versus global commodity pressures; key watchpoints include Q4 GDP releases and January 2025 Politburo signals for reflation catalysts.

question: Will Matt Mahan advance from the 2026 California Governor primary election?
description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve to “Yes” If the listed candidate advances from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

With Governor Gavin Newsom term-limited, the open race features Democrats Tony Thurmond (12%), Eleni Kounalakis (11%), and Toni Atkins (9%) vying for the second spot, while Republicans like Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco trail at 7-10% amid California's Democratic dominance. Porter's October campaign launch bolstered her momentum post-Senate bid, but fluid fields and undecided voters (over 50%) leave room for shifts. Key upcoming events include candidate filing deadlines in early 2025 and potential endorsements from Newsom or Harris allies.

question: Will Chelsea win the 2025-2026 FA Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2025-2026 FA Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2025-2026 FA Cup per the rules of the tournament (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2025-2026 FA Cup is cancelled, postponed after June 30, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Football Association (https://www.thefa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal commands the top spot in trader consensus at 35% implied probability for the 2025-26 FA Cup winner, driven by their sustained Premier League title contention, robust squad depth, and knockout pedigree under Mikel Arteta. Manchester City follows at 23.5%, buoyed by recent treble success and Pep Guardiola's tactical edge despite a midseason league dip, while Chelsea's 21.5% reflects their upward trajectory with key returns like Nkunku and Enzo Maresca's cohesion. Liverpool sits at 15%, tempered by a packed schedule amid their dominant league lead under Arne Slot. Lower tiers like West Ham (6.1%), promotion-chasing Leeds (5.6%), and Southampton (3.1%) draw support from cup upset history and squad reinforcements, with Port Vale (0.6%) as a classic longshot; recent winter transfers and form momentum continue shaping these early futures markets.

question: Abilene Christian Wildcats vs. Tarleton State Texans
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for January 26 at 7:00 PM ET:

If the Abilene Christian Wildcats win, the market will resolve to "Abilene Christian Wildcats".

If the Tarleton State Texans win, the market will resolve to "Tarleton State Texans".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Abilene Christian Wildcats vs. Tarleton State Texans

eventMetadata.context_description: Tarleton State Texans' dominant home-court advantage and superior recent form anchor their 76.5% implied probability against Abilene Christian Wildcats. The Texans boast a 7-2 record in their last nine games, including a convincing 78-65 win over Abilene Christian earlier this season, fueled by standout guard play and top-150 national rebounding margins. Abilene Christian limps in at 3-6 over their past nine, hampered by poor shooting efficiency (41% FG) and key forward DJ Richards sidelined by a lingering ankle sprain per official reports. Head-to-head trends favor Tarleton's physicality in WAC matchups, with traders pricing in the Wildcats' road woes (1-5 away) and fatigue from a compressed schedule.

question: Space FDV above $15M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Space's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Space (https://x.com/intodotspace) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from edgeX , however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will the South African Reserve Bank decrease the repo rate after the March meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the repo rate resulting from the South African Reserve Bank’s March Monetary Policy Committee meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the South African Reserve Bank after its March 26, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official South African Reserve Bank meeting schedule: https://www.resbank.co.za/en/home/calendar

This market may resolve as soon as the South African Reserve Bank's statement for their March 26, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the repo rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: South African Reserve Bank Decision in March?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the repo rate resulting from the South African Reserve Bank’s March Monetary Policy Committee meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the South African Reserve Bank after its March 26, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official South African Reserve Bank meeting schedule: https://www.resbank.co.za/en/home/calendar

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no change (84%) in the South African Reserve Bank's repo rate at its March 21 meeting, reflecting cooling headline inflation at 5.3% YoY in January—within the 3-6% target—coupled with core inflation dipping to 4.5%. Weak GDP growth (0.1% Q4 contraction) and ongoing load-shedding pressures argue against hikes (just 2.6% implied odds), while downside risks from rand volatility and subdued demand limit cut expectations (11%). Recent upside pressures from food prices and electricity tariffs have steadied sentiment around the steady 8.25% benchmark, with traders eyeing tomorrow's February CPI release as a pivotal pre-decision catalyst amid global easing signals from the Fed.

question: Jack Smith charged by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of former Department of Justice Special Prosecutor Jack Smith by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Jack Smith charged by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of former Department of Justice Special Prosecutor Jack Smith by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no charges against Special Counsel Jack Smith by March 31, driven by the absence of any official announcements, indictments, or investigations from the incoming Trump administration or current DOJ. Recent developments include Smith's November 25 motions to dismiss federal cases against President-elect Trump—citing DOJ policy on sitting presidents—which were granted without prejudice, reducing immediate friction but not erasing prior tensions. Trump's allies, like FBI nominee Kash Patel, have signaled potential scrutiny of Smith's probes, yet logistical hurdles loom: Senate confirmations for Attorney General nominee Pam Bondi and others precede any prosecutorial action post-January 20 inauguration. Markets price in the short timeline and high evidentiary bar for federal charges, reflecting skepticism on rapid escalation.

question: Will Tesla release Optimus by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tesla releases a humanoid, bipedal robot (such as Optimus) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying product is a humanoid, bipedal robot intended for consumer use and newly introduced. Non-humanoid robotics, accessories, internal factory deployments, employee-only programs, and partner/enterprise pilots do not qualify.

To be considered “released,” the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the timeframe via an official Tesla consumer channel (e.g., tesla.com) with a live checkout or paid preorder/deposit. Announcements, unveilings, demos, or waitlists with “register interest” pages without payment do not suffice. Availability in any region counts if it is open to the general public.

The primary resolution source will be official statements and materials from Tesla.

events.title: Will Tesla release Optimus by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tesla releases a humanoid, bipedal robot (such as Optimus) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying product is a humanoid, bipedal robot intended for consumer use and newly introduced. Non-humanoid robotics, accessories, internal factory deployments, employee-only programs, and partner/enterprise pilots do not qualify.

To be considered “released,” the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the timeframe via an official Tesla consumer channel (e.g., tesla.com) with a live checkout or paid preorder/deposit. Announcements, unveilings, demos, or waitlists with “register interest” pages without payment do not suffice. Availability in any region counts if it is open to the general public.

The primary resolution source will be official statements and materials from Tesla.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla's Q3 earnings call on October 23 confirmed CEO Elon Musk's timeline for Optimus humanoid robot: limited internal production in 2025 for factory use, with external sales not until 2026, driving trader consensus to price a mere 12% implied probability of any release by year-end 2024. Recent We, Robot event demos showcased walking and folding shirts but relied on teleoperation, underscoring pre-commercial status amid skepticism over autonomy claims. Competitive pressure from Figure AI and Boston Dynamics accelerates Tesla's ambitions, yet no shipped units or firm milestones exist before Q1 2025 updates, leaving room for surprise announcements at future events like the next shareholder meeting. Traders should watch for production prototypes in Tesla factories as a key resolution trigger.

question: Will Brazil’s Annual Inflation in 2026 be less than 3.00%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in Brazil over the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics (IBGE).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Extended National Consumer Price Index (IPCA) during the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly IBGE report.

The resolution source for this market will be the IBGE Extended National Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 12, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find the relevant figure by locating the report for December 2026 on the Press Releases page (https://agenciadenoticias.ibge.gov.br/en/agencia-press-room.html), locating the Period-Rate table and finding the IPCA growth rate figure in the column labeled "Rate" and the Row labeled “Cumulative in the year / 12 Months”. Changes in the IBGE’s reporting format will not disqualify a published relevant figure from counting.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly IBGE IPCA news release which reports inflation during 12-month periods to two decimal points (e.g. 4.26%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.ibge.gov.br/en/calendar.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Brazil's 2026 annual inflation in the 4.00-4.49% band at 48% implied probability, closely trailed by 3.50-3.99% at 34.5%, signaling expectations of moderated disinflation from current levels toward the Banco Central do Brasil's 3% target with ±1.5% tolerance. Recent IPCA readings eased to 4.42% YoY in September—down from 4.83% in July—bolstered by tighter monetary policy, with the Selic rate held at 10.75% in October amid the Copom's hawkish pause despite cuts earlier this year. BCB's Focus survey median forecast for 2026 hovers near 3.5%, but fiscal expansion risks under the current administration and volatile commodity prices cap aggressive easing bets, positioning higher bands as resilient amid election-year uncertainties in 2026.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Extended National Consumer Price Index (IPCA) during the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly IBGE report.

The resolution source for this market will be the IBGE Extended National Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 12, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find the relevant figure by locating the report for December 2026 on the Press Releases page (https://agenciadenoticias.ibge.gov.br/en/agencia-press-room.html), locating the Period-Rate table and finding the IPCA growth rate figure in the column labeled "Rate" and the Row labeled “Cumulative in the year / 12 Months”. Changes in the IBGE’s reporting format will not disqualify a published relevant figure from counting.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly IBGE IPCA news release which reports inflation during 12-month periods to two decimal points (e.g. 4.26%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.ibge.gov.br/en/calendar.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Brazil's 2026 annual inflation in the 4.00-4.49% band at 48% implied probability, closely trailed by 3.50-3.99% at 34.5%, signaling expectations of moderated disinflation from current levels toward the Banco Central do Brasil's 3% target with ±1.5% tolerance. Recent IPCA readings eased to 4.42% YoY in September—down from 4.83% in July—bolstered by tighter monetary policy, with the Selic rate held at 10.75% in October amid the Copom's hawkish pause despite cuts earlier this year. BCB's Focus survey median forecast for 2026 hovers near 3.5%, but fiscal expansion risks under the current administration and volatile commodity prices cap aggressive easing bets, positioning higher bands as resilient amid election-year uncertainties in 2026.

question: Will world GDP growth be ≤2.9% in 2026?
description:
This Market will resolve according to the estimate of the World GDP growth rate (Real GDP, annual percent change) reported by the International Monetary Fund in the World Economic Outlook Update released between the October 2026 and April 2027 editions of the World Economic Outlook, expected to be released in January 2027. 

The relevant figure may be found in the table titled “World Economic Outlook Growth Projections” under “Estimate” in the row “World Output” and the column “2026”. Changes in the IMF’s World Economic Outlook reporting format will not disqualify a published figure from counting.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.imf.org/en/publications/weo

If no estimate of the 2026 annual percent change in world real GDP is released in a World Economic Outlook Update between the October 2026 and April 2027 editions of the World Economic Outlook, this market will resolve according to the published figure for 2026 annual percent change in World real GDP in the April 2027 edition of the World Economic Outlook. If no such figure is published by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

If multiple World Economic Outlook Updates are released between the October 2026 and April 2027 editions of the World Economic Outlook, this market will resolve based on the first such publication which includes an estimate of the 2026 annual percent change in World GDP. Projections of World GDP, however, will not be considered.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following release or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports annual percent change in world real GDP to one decimal point (e.g. 3.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.title: 2026 World GDP Growth

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket's trader consensus on 2026 world GDP growth shows a razor-thin race, with ≤2.9% holding a 31.5% implied probability ahead of 3.6% at 29.8% and 3.4% at 26.8%, reflecting deep uncertainty in global forecasts. The primary catalyst is divergent institutional baselines: IMF's October 2024 World Economic Outlook projects a stable 3.2-3.3% extension from 2025's 3.3% amid resilient US expansion, while World Bank's June outlook flags sub-3% risks at 2.7% from weak emerging market demand and China's property woes. Differentiating factors hinge on trade tensions, Fed rate path, and Beijing stimulus efficacy; key watches include IMF's January 2025 revision and Q4 global PMIs, as capital-weighted odds tilt toward deceleration.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports annual percent change in world real GDP to one decimal point (e.g. 3.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.title: 2026 World GDP Growth
events.description:
This Market will resolve according to the estimate of the World GDP growth rate (Real GDP, annual percent change) reported by the International Monetary Fund in the World Economic Outlook Update released between the October 2026 and April 2027 editions of the World Economic Outlook, expected to be released in January 2027. 

The relevant figure may be found in the table titled “World Economic Outlook Growth Projections” under “Estimate” in the row “World Output” and the column “2026”. Changes in the IMF’s World Economic Outlook reporting format will not disqualify a published figure from counting.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.imf.org/en/publications/weo

If no estimate of the 2026 annual percent change in world real GDP is released in a World Economic Outlook Update between the October 2026 and April 2027 editions of the World Economic Outlook, this market will resolve according to the published figure for 2026 annual percent change in World real GDP in the April 2027 edition of the World Economic Outlook. If no such figure is published by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

If multiple World Economic Outlook Updates are released between the October 2026 and April 2027 editions of the World Economic Outlook, this market will resolve based on the first such publication which includes an estimate of the 2026 annual percent change in World GDP. Projections of World GDP, however, will not be considered.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following release or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports annual percent change in world real GDP to one decimal point (e.g. 3.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket's trader consensus on 2026 world GDP growth shows a razor-thin race, with ≤2.9% holding a 31.5% implied probability ahead of 3.6% at 29.8% and 3.4% at 26.8%, reflecting deep uncertainty in global forecasts. The primary catalyst is divergent institutional baselines: IMF's October 2024 World Economic Outlook projects a stable 3.2-3.3% extension from 2025's 3.3% amid resilient US expansion, while World Bank's June outlook flags sub-3% risks at 2.7% from weak emerging market demand and China's property woes. Differentiating factors hinge on trade tensions, Fed rate path, and Beijing stimulus efficacy; key watches include IMF's January 2025 revision and Q4 global PMIs, as capital-weighted odds tilt toward deceleration.

question: Ledger IPO closing market cap above $1B?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the official closing price for Ledger's market capitalization on its first trading day is above the value specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Ledger’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading.

question: Virginia Cavaliers vs. Wake Forest Demon Deacons (W)
description:
In the upcoming WBB game, scheduled for January 29 at 6:00 PM ET:

If the Virginia Cavaliers win, the market will resolve to "Virginia Cavaliers".

If the Wake Forest Demon Deacons win, the market will resolve to "Wake Forest Demon Deacons".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

question: Bank of Japan decreases interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?
description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for April is scheduled to be released on April 28, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's April 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 59% probability of a 25 basis point rate hike by the Bank of Japan at its April 25-26 meeting, propelled by persistent yen weakness near 153 against the dollar and hotter-than-expected March core CPI at 2.8%, fueling inflation persistence above the 2% target. Robust spring wage negotiations, with major firms agreeing to over 5% increases, bolster expectations for policy normalization following March's end to negative rates. No-change odds at 38% reflect Governor Ueda's cautious tone amid global uncertainties, while outsized hikes remain improbable at 1.2% due to BoJ's gradualist approach. Upcoming Tokyo CPI on April 19 could shift these market-implied odds further.

question: Will South African inflation be less than 2.6% in 2026?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Africa over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (Y/Y, % change), as reported by Statistics South Africa.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) during the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly Statistics South Africa report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Statistics South Africa Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 20, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find the relevant figure by locating the report for December 2026 on the Publications page (https://www.statssa.gov.za/?page_id=1859), selecting the report, and selecting “Key Findings (Summary)". The relevant figure will be the figure for annual consumer price inflation (Y/Y, %) in the relevant month. Changes in Statistics South Africa’s reporting format will not disqualify a published relevant figure from counting.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Statistics South Africa Consumer Price Index report which reports inflation during 12-month periods to one decimal point (e.g. 3.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.statssa.gov.za/?page_id=1874

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing South Africa’s 2026 annual inflation in a tight contest, with 3.2-3.5% (29%) edging >5.0% (28%) and 4.7-5.0% (27%), reflecting SARB’s 3-6% target band amid recent CPI cooling to 4.4% in August from 5.1% in June. Driving this balance, September’s repo rate cut to 8% signals easing pressures from a firmer rand (USDZAR ~17.40) and subdued food costs, favoring sub-4% paths per SARB and IMF median forecasts around 4.3%. Yet, competitive upside risks from Eskom tariff hikes, wage settlements, and rand volatility—evident in 2024’s power crises—bolster >5% odds, with November’s MPC meeting as the key differentiator for sustained disinflation versus rebound.

question: Will SpaceX IPO by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If SpaceX is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elon Musk's steadfast opposition to a near-term SpaceX IPO remains the dominant force suppressing trader optimism on Polymarket, with market-implied odds for 2025 or sooner languishing below 20% amid his October 2024 reaffirmation that Starlink must first reach revenue stability for a potential spin-off listing. Recent Starship test successes and a $350 billion valuation boost investor hype but underscore Musk's preference for private control to fund ambitious Mars goals, contrasting public peers like Rocket Lab. Watch Q1 2025 Starlink updates and any SEC filings, as regulatory scrutiny on SpaceX's government contracts could indirectly pressure liquidity events.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If SpaceX is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elon Musk's steadfast opposition to a near-term SpaceX IPO remains the dominant force suppressing trader optimism on Polymarket, with market-implied odds for 2025 or sooner languishing below 20% amid his October 2024 reaffirmation that Starlink must first reach revenue stability for a potential spin-off listing. Recent Starship test successes and a $350 billion valuation boost investor hype but underscore Musk's preference for private control to fund ambitious Mars goals, contrasting public peers like Rocket Lab. Watch Q1 2025 Starlink updates and any SEC filings, as regulatory scrutiny on SpaceX's government contracts could indirectly pressure liquidity events.

question: Will the US conduct a cyberattack on Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States conducts a major cyberattack against Iran between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying “major cyberattack” is a cyber operation attributable to the United States that causes significant disruption, damage, or unauthorized access affecting core Iranian national systems—such as critical infrastructure, state institutions, the financial system (including major state operated banks), defense networks, or nuclear facilities.

To qualify, the cyberattack must be either (i) officially acknowledged by the U.S. government (or an authorized U.S. government representative), or (ii) confirmed by a broad consensus of credible reporting attributing the operation to the United States. 

Smaller-scale incidents—such as attacks on individual companies or organizations that do not target core national systems—will not qualify.

Cyber incidents that are thwarted without significant disruption, damage, or unauthorized access will not qualify.

Cyber operations that are directly related to, coordinated with, or executed as part of kinetic military action (including, but not limited to, airstrikes, missile/drone strikes, raids, ground incursions, or other physical combat operations) will not qualify, even if they cause significant cyber effects.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government and/or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 92.5% implied probability against a US cyberattack on Iran by March 31, primarily due to repeated official statements from the Biden administration affirming no offensive military involvement amid Israel-Iran tensions. Following Iran's October 2024 missile barrages on Israel, US forces aided defensive intercepts but avoided escalation, with Pentagon officials emphasizing restraint to prevent broader regional conflict. No verified intelligence or announcements signal cyber preparations from US Cyber Command, contrasting past joint operations like Stuxnet. Incoming Trump administration rhetoric focuses on deterrence without specifying cyber actions, while diplomatic channels remain open, underscoring low escalation risk ahead of the deadline.

question: Will SpaceX's market cap be less than $1.0T at market close on IPO day?
description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a SpaceX IPO closing above $2 trillion at 57.5% implied probability, propelled by Starship's accelerating test cadence and Starlink's subscriber surge past 4 million, which underscore potential for dominant satellite internet market share and reusable launch economics. Recent Flight 5 success in October 2024, featuring the first booster catch, bolsters confidence in rapid iteration toward orbital refueling and Mars ambitions, potentially multiplying current $210 billion private valuation multiples akin to Tesla's post-autonomy hype. While Elon Musk cautions against near-term IPO until Starship maturity, traders price in regulatory tailwinds from FCC spectrum approvals and dismiss delays, with lower brackets and no-IPO-by-2028 odds below 5% reflecting optimism for a 2026-2027 listing amid intensifying competition from Blue Origin and ULA.

question: Will there be at least 7500 measles cases in the U.S. in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026 according to the CDC case counter between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

events.title: Measles cases in U.S. in 2026?
events.description:
